---
name: obligatory_java_python_migration_practices
description: Apply Java-to-Python migration good practices for behaviour-preserving, idiomatic, and maintainable migrations. Always apply when any Java class, method, or module is being translated or re-implemented in Python, even partially.
---

# Obligatory Java-to-Python Migration Practices

## Core principle
**Preserve behaviour first.** Never refactor and migrate at the same time — migrate faithfully first, then improve.

## Step-by-step migration checklist

### Step 1 — Tests before code
- Migrate or write equivalent Python tests **before** touching the implementation
- Verify the Python tests match the Java test coverage (see `src/test/java/HelloWorldTest.java` as the baseline)
- Use `pytest` as the default test framework

### Step 2 — Module structure
- Translate each Java class into its own small, focused Python module (file)
- **Do not** create one giant Python file that mirrors the entire Java class hierarchy
- Inner records / inner classes → either separate dataclasses or a dedicated `models.py`

### Step 3 — Type mapping

| Java construct | Python equivalent |
|----------------|------------------|
| `record Greeting(String r, String m)` | `@dataclass(frozen=True)` class |
| `sealed interface TimeOfDay` | `ABC` base class or `Union` type alias |
| `String` | `str` |
| `int` | `int` |
| `void` | `None` return annotation |
| Checked exceptions | Specific `Exception` subclasses |
| `switch` expression | `match` statement (Python 3.10+) or `if/elif` chain |

### Step 4 — Naming conventions
| Java | Python |
|------|--------|
| `camelCase` methods | `snake_case` functions |
| `PascalCase` classes | `PascalCase` classes ✅ (same) |
| `UPPER_SNAKE` constants | `UPPER_SNAKE` constants ✅ (same) |
| `getX()` / `setX(v)` | `@property` / `@x.setter` (only where meaningful) |

### Step 5 — Exception handling
- Replace Java's checked-exception declarations with Python-specific exception types
- Raise `ValueError` for validation errors (equivalent to `IllegalArgumentException`)
- Document raised exceptions in docstrings

### Step 6 — Standard library preference
- Prefer Python standard library over third-party packages for simple tasks
- `java.time.LocalDate` → `datetime.date`
- `java.time.Month` → `datetime.date.month` (integer 1–12) or `calendar.month_name`
- Text blocks / multi-line strings → Python triple-quoted strings `"""`

### Step 7 — Quality gates (run after each migration batch)
```
pytest                    # all tests must pass
flake8 src/               # PEP 8 compliance
mypy src/                 # type checking
```

## AI comment compliance during migration
When creating new Python files or classes during migration, apply `obligatory_ai_generation_comment_compliance`:
```python
# generated by AI in Github cloud
```
